rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEGAN LOUISE FURTH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Confirming Estate Administrator'S Assertion Of Decedent'S Attorney-Client Privilege
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for July 05, 2006. Motion For Order Confirming Estate Administrator's Assertion Of Decedent's Attorney-Client Privilege is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as it seeks a finding that the documents already produced to Mr. Koplowitz are privileged. As any privilege might attach to those documents, it has been waived by their production. The court does not find that those documents were inadvertently produced. The motion is denied as it seeks a finding that all testimony from Mr. Walker and Mr. Kaiser is privileged. The court finds that Frederick Furth, Donna Furth, and Jerzy Sokol all claim through the deceased, and there is no privilege that attaches to any communication relevant to an issue between those parties pursuant to E.C. Section 957. By this order, the court makes no finding as to the relevance, and therefore the application of these findings to specific questions that may be posed to Mr. Walker and Mr. Kaiser or any other witness. The court would be guided by Paley v Superior Court, 137 Cal.App.2d 450 in determining whether particular questions are subject to a claim of privilege as they may be related to the administration of this estate. = (612/EAH)
